lineage: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
The Re-Formation Which is Now Being Effected, Compared with the Perfection of the Life to Come.
title: The Re-Formation Which is Now Being Effected, Compared with the Perfection of the Life to Come.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Chapter 38 [XXIII.]—The Re-Formation Which is Now Being Effected, Compared with the Perfection of the Life to Come.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: But what is this change, and how great, in comparison with the perfect eminence which is then to be realized?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: The apostle applies some sort of illustration, derived from well-known things, to these indescribable things, comparing the period of childhood with the age of manhood.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: “When I was a child,” says he, “I used to speak as a child, to understand as a child, to think as a child; but when I became a man, I put aside childish things.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: He then immediately explains why he said this in these words: “For now we see by means of a mirror, darkly but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known.”
